deep_dive.title: Multi-Scale Markov Switching GARCH
deep_dive.abstract: Financial volatility exhibits substantial non-stationarity, making single-regime models inadequate for characterising changing market conditions. This paper proposes a triple-timeframe Markov-Switching GARCH (MS-GARCH) framework for volatility regime detection in EUR/USD across daily, four-hour, and hourly horizons. Three independent AR(1)-MS-GARCH models are estimated to capture macro, meso, and micro regime dynamics, while Filardo-style time-varying transition probabilities (TVTP) are incorporated at the shorter horizons through composite stress indicators. The resulting regime probabilities are combined through an outer-product construction into a 27-state cross-scale probability tensor. Using EUR/USD data from 2015-2025, the framework produces statistically distinct Calm, Turbulent, and Crisis regimes and achieves superior out-of-sample volatility forecasting performance relative to a conventional GARCH benchmark. The results suggest that volatility dynamics contain meaningful structure across multiple timescales and that modelling these scales separately provides a more informative representation of market conditions than a single-timescale approach.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: multi-scale Markov switching GARCH for volatility regime detection in EUR/USD
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 多时间尺度马尔可夫转换GARCH模型用于欧元/美元波动率区制检测
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 多时间尺度马尔可夫转换GARCH模型用于欧元/美元波动率区制检测

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a triple-timeframe Markov-Switching GARCH with time-varying transition probabilities to model volatility regimes across daily, 4-hour, and hourly horizons.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 论文针对金融波动率非平稳性，提出三时间尺度的马尔可夫转换GARCH框架。在日度、4小时和1小时频率上分别估计AR(1)-MS-GARCH模型，并在较短时间尺度引入时变转换概率（TVTP），通过外积构造得到27状态跨尺度概率张量。实验显示该方法能清晰区分EUR/USD的平静、动荡和危机三种波动率区制，为多尺度风险管理提供了新工具。

deep_dive.title: A new decomposition approach to modeling financial returns: Conditioning sign on magnitude
deep_dive.abstract: Changes in volatility contain valuable information about the likelihood of positive versus negative returns. We propose a new approach to modeling financial returns that exploits this insight by decomposing returns into sign and magnitude (absolute value) components, with magnitude closely related to volatility. The joint distribution used to compute expected returns combines a model for the marginal distribution of magnitude with a model for the distribution of the sign, conditional on the contemporaneous magnitude. Unlike traditional linear predictive regressions, this decomposition framework captures nonlinear predictability in return dynamics. An out-of-sample forecasting evaluation using monthly U.S. stock market excess returns demonstrates substantial statistical and economic gains relative to linear regression and complete subset regression, while delivering performance that is competitive with copula-based return-decomposition methods and other nonlinear benchmarks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Decomposes returns into sign and magnitude for volatility-related predictability with out-of-sample evaluation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 将收益分解为符号与幅度捕捉波动率相关可预测性并包含样本外评估
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 将收益分解为符号与幅度捕捉波动率相关可预测性并包含样本外评估
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 将收益分解为符号与幅度捕捉波动率相关可预测性并包含样本外评估
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a return decomposition model conditioning sign on magnitude, capturing nonlinear volatility-related predictability with out-of-sample forecasting.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该文提出了一种新的金融收益分解方法，将收益拆分为符号和幅度两个部分，其中幅度与波动率紧密相关。通过构建幅度边际分布和给定幅度下符号的条件分布，捕捉了收益动态中的非线性可预测性。在对美国股市月度超额收益的样本外预测评估中，该方法展现了比传统线性预测回归更好的性能，为利用波动率信息改进收益预测提供了新视角，具有经济解释力。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该文提出了一种新的金融收益分解方法，将收益拆分为符号和幅度两个部分，其中幅度与波动率紧密相关。通过构建幅度边际分布和给定幅度下符号的条件分布，捕捉了收益动态中的非线性可预测性。在对美国股市月度超额收益的样本外预测评估中，该方法展现了比传统线性预测回归更好的性能，为利用波动率信息改进收益预测提供了新视角，具有经济解释力。

deep_dive.title: Derivative-Informed Operator Learning for Finance: On-the-Fly Greeks, Surfaces, Hedging, and Control
deep_dive.abstract: Financial decision systems require fast surrogate models for pricing, calibration, hedging, XVA, stress testing, and portfolio optimization. Standard neural surrogates reproduce prices or risk quantities, but downstream tasks depend as much on derivatives: deltas, vegas, curve and credit-spread sensitivities, exposure and objective gradients. We formulate a derivative-informed operator-learning framework in which the learned map -- a neural operator, random-feature operator, or finite-dimensional surrogate -- is trained both to match a high-fidelity pricing or risk operator and to match directional Fréchet derivatives generated on the fly. The framework combines operator learning, adjoint algorithmic differentiation, tangent sensitivity equations, random sketching of Jacobian actions, and no-arbitrage constraints. We derive error bounds showing derivative accuracy controls local stress errors, hedging error, and optimizer instability, and that discrete-time hedging error is also governed by second-order (gamma) accuracy. A Black--Scholes network over eight seeds shows a tuned derivative weight cuts vega error by 40\% and delta error by 15\% while modestly improving prices, but not an unsupervised second-order Greek. Heston and Bates random-feature experiments reduce stochastic-volatility and jump-parameter sensitivity errors by 60--76\%. A random-feature DeepONet/Galerkin operator mapping instantaneous-volatility curves to dense price surfaces reduces out-of-sample JVP error by 44\% and price RMSE by 23\% over eight seeds; it also shows derivative consistency alone does not remove no-arbitrage violations, so economic constraints must be imposed explicitly. The framework gives a disciplined route from value-only surrogates to derivative-aware engines that output differentiable instruments for hedging, risk, and control.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Learns financial operators for Greeks, surfaces, and hedging, directly relevant to options and volatility
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 学习金融算子以生成希腊值、曲面和对冲，直接涉及期权与波动率

deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 学习金融算子以生成希腊值、曲面和对冲，直接涉及期权与波动率
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a derivative-informed operator learning framework for fast pricing, Greeks, and hedging, applicable to volatility surfaces.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该文针对金融决策系统中快速定价、校准、对冲及投资组合优化的需求，提出了导数信息算子学习框架。通过训练神经算子或随机特征算子同时匹配高保真定价或风险算子及其方向Fréchet导数，实现了希腊值和敏感性的实时生成。该方法可应用于波动率曲面、对冲和风险管控等多个下游任务，为期权市场中的高效计算提供了通用工具，尤其适合高频环境下的动态风险管理。

deep_dive.title: Forecasting of volatility and risk premia in electricity markets
deep_dive.abstract: We study forecasting of the realized covariation in electricity markets. The realized covariation in this context is a matrix-valued representation of the latent infinite-dimensional covariance operator and a parsimonious matrix-HAR type model is constructed to facilitate estimation. We test the model on one-week ahead forecasts of the weekly realized covariation and find that the inclusion of longer time horizons and renewable generation information adds important predictive power. We also investigate the prediction of risk premia in electricity forward markets and find that our variance forecasts provide substantially improved forecasts of spread risk premia compared to standard methods relying on backward looking volatility.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: volatility forecasting in electricity markets using a matrix-HAR model and realized covariation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用矩阵HAR模型和已实现协方差进行电力市场波动率预测

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Forecasts weekly realized covariation in electricity markets with a parsimonious matrix-HAR model, including longer horizons and renewables data, and improves risk premium predictions.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 论文研究电力市场已实现协方差的预测，将无限维协方差算子表示为矩阵值过程，构建简洁的矩阵HAR模型。模型纳入更长时间尺度和可再生能源信息，在一周前预测中显著提升预测力。此外，方差预测显著改善了电力远期市场的风险溢价预测，优于传统的后顾波动率方法。该框架可推广至其他资产端的波动率预测与风险管理。

deep_dive.title: Distributionally robust shortfall risk portfolio model with moment ambiguity sets
deep_dive.abstract: This paper employs shortfall risk to measure portfolio risk. Assume stock returns follow polynomial relations with their influence factors. We propose a moment-based distributionally robust optimization (DRO) shortfall risk portfolio model. For piecewise linear loss functions, we show that this DRO model can be transformed into a tractable linear conic optimization problem with nonnegative polynomial cones. A Moment-SOS relaxation algorithm is proposed to solve the transformed problem. Its finite and asymptotic convergence properties are studied. For optimizers computed from our algorithm, we give convenient conditions verify their global optimality for the original DRO problem. Numerical experiments involving real stock market data are given to show the efficiency of our approach.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 采用短缺风险度量的分布鲁棒投资组合优化

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a moment-based DRO shortfall risk portfolio model, transformable to linear conic optimization, with Moment-SOS algorithm and real stock experiments.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出基于矩模糊集的分布鲁棒短缺风险投资组合模型，将分段线性损失函数下的模型转化为线性锥优化问题，并设计Moment-SOS松弛算法求解，利用真实股票数据验证了模型的有效性和算法收敛性。

deep_dive.title: OrderGrad: Optimizing Beyond the Mean with Order-Statistic Policy Gradient Estimation
deep_dive.abstract: Policy-gradient methods usually optimize expected return, but many real world applications care about distributional properties of returns: tail risk, outlier robustness, or best-of-K discovery. We introduce OrderGrad, a family of likelihood-ratio and reparameterization gradient estimators for order-statistic objectives. OrderGrad optimizes finite-sample L-statistics, i.e., weighted averages of sorted rewards or costs, recovering objectives such as VaR, CVaR, trimmed means, medians, and top-m/best-of-K criteria by changing only the rank weights. For any fixed sample size and rank-weight vector, OrderGrad provides an unbiased gradient estimator for the corresponding order-statistic objective. The method is implemented as a simple reward transformation that can then be used in an otherwise standard policy-gradient or reparameterized update. We study the resulting estimator's variance behavior and evaluate it on tasks where mean optimization is mismatched to the deployment objective, including LLM math post-training and other tasks. OrderGrad provides a unified, plug-and-play route to risk-averse, robust, and exploratory learning.   Code: https://github.com/paavo5/ordergrad
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: directly optimizes order-statistic objectives including VaR and CVaR (tail risk)
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 直接优化包括VaR和CVaR在内的序统计量目标（尾部风险）
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 直接优化包括VaR和CVaR在内的序统计量目标（尾部风险）
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 直接优化包括VaR和CVaR在内的序统计量目标（尾部风险）
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: OrderGrad proposes policy gradient estimators for order-statistic objectives like VaR and CVaR, providing unbiased gradients and enabling optimization of tail risk in RL settings.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 许多决策场景中需关注回报分布尾部而非均值，序统计量目标（如VaR、CVaR）至关重要。OrderGrad提供基于似然比和重参数化的策略梯度估计器族，通过改变秩权重灵活优化有限样本L-统计量。该方法为任意序统计量目标提供无偏梯度，在强化学习任务中有效管理尾部风险和异常值。实验表明，OrderGrad在控制极端风险和发现最佳策略方面均优于仅优化均值的方法，且实现简单。

quick_skim.title: From Long News to Accurate Forecast: Importance-Aware Fusion and PRM-Guided Reflection for Time Series Forecasting
quick_skim.abstract: Incorporating news into time series forecasting is appealing because news can reveal abrupt exogenous events that historical values alone cannot recover. However, existing LLM-based news-forecasting pipelines face two practical limitations: relevant news articles often exceed the model's context window, and iterative retrieval of supplementary news is typically unguided, leading to redundant updates and slow convergence. We address these issues with a novel framework that combines importance-aware news compression and process-level retrieval supervision. First, we train an importance reward model that estimates the forecasting utility of each article and uses this signal to allocate compression budgets during sequential pairwise fusion, preserving informative content within a fixed context limit. Second, we introduce a process reward model (PRM) that ranks multiple supplementary-news candidates conditioned on the current error profile and the history of previously selected articles, replacing one-shot blind retrieval with quality-controlled selection. Both components are trained offline using historical data with ground truth; inference uses the frozen filtering logic and compression modules without any reflection loop. Experiments on finance, energy, traffic, and bitcoin forecasting benchmarks show that our method improves prediction accuracy over strong baselines, significantly reduces the number of refinement iterations compared to the iterative baseline, and remains effective when relevant articles span thousands of tokens.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: time series forecasting with LLM-based news fusion, directly applicable to financial volatility
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于LLM新闻融合的时间序列预测方法，可直接用于金融波动率预测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于LLM新闻融合的时间序列预测方法，可直接用于金融波动率预测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于LLM新闻融合的时间序列预测方法，可直接用于金融波动率预测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a framework that uses importance-aware news compression and process-level reward model guidance to improve long-news time series forecasting.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 论文针对现有LLM新闻预测流程中长新闻溢出上下文窗口和迭代检索无监督的问题，提出重要性感知压缩和过程级检索监督的结合框架。首先训练重要性奖励模型来分配压缩预算，然后用过程奖励模型指导新闻检索，避免冗余更新。该方法在时间序列预测中提升了新闻融合的效率和准确性，尤其适用于金融等领域的突发外生事件预测。

quick_skim.title: Rethinking Post-Training Recipes for Multimodal Time-Series Forecasting
quick_skim.abstract: Time-Series Foundation Models (TSFMs) excel at zero-shot unimodal forecasting using numerical data, but unlike LLMs they cannot consume multimodal, non-numerical context that often shape real-world trajectories. In this work, we bridge this gap and argue for a multimodal time-series forecasting approach that post-trains LLMs to act as context-guided revisors over strong numerical TSFM priors. We introduce PostTime, a post-training recipe combining Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) and Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR), along with a methodology to generate automated reasoning traces for forecast revisions. PostTime teaches an LLM to generate context-conditioned forecast interventions -- decisions to revise, preserve, or ignore the TSFM prior based on the multimodal context. We evaluate this approach on the TimesX multimodal forecasting benchmark using a Gemma-3-4B LLM and TimesFM-2.5 TSFM, and show that it significantly outperforms standalone TSFMs, LLM-only baselines, and existing multimodal forecasting approaches.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Proposes PostTime, a multimodal time-series forecasting approach using LLMs to revise numerical TSFM forecasts, applicable to financial series when incorporating textual context.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出 PostTime，一种多模态时间序列预测方法，利用 LLM 对数值型 TSFM 预测进行修正，当融入文本上下文时可应用于金融序列。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出 PostTime，一种多模态时间序列预测方法，利用 LLM 对数值型 TSFM 预测进行修正，当融入文本上下文时可应用于金融序列。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出 PostTime，一种多模态时间序列预测方法，利用 LLM 对数值型 TSFM 预测进行修正，当融入文本上下文时可应用于金融序列。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: PostTime fine-tunes LLMs with SFT and RLVR to generate context-guided interventions on base time-series forecasts, improving accuracy by leveraging multimodal signals.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对时间序列基座模型无法利用多模态语境的局限，本文提出 PostTime，通过监督微调和带有可验证奖励的强化学习，训练大语言模型作为数值预测的上下文指导修正器。模型自主决定是否修改、保留或忽略基座预测，在多个基准上显著提升预测性能。该框架为将非数值信息融入金融时间序列预测提供了新途径。

quick_skim.title: Wasserstein Exponential Smoothing
quick_skim.abstract: Exponential smoothing (ES) often outperforms other techniques in time series forecasting across a wide range of data-generating processes. While ES has traditionally been applied to time series in $\mathbb{R}$, this paper extends the methodology to distributional time series, where each observation is a probability distribution on $\mathbb{R}$. The primary contribution of this work is twofold. First, we propose a principled and intuitive generalization of ES within the Wasserstein space, which retains the exceptional parsimony of classical ES. Second, we theoretically and empirically demonstrate that the smoothing parameter can be consistently estimated by minimizing a Wasserstein distance. Applications to distributional time series of high-frequency financial returns and household electricity demands confirm the practical effectiveness of our Wasserstein ES model.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Extends exponential smoothing to distributional time series of high-frequency financial returns, enabling parsimonious volatility forecasting.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 将指数平滑扩展到高频金融收益的分布时间序列，支持简约的波动率预测。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 将指数平滑扩展到高频金融收益的分布时间序列，支持简约的波动率预测。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 将指数平滑扩展到高频金融收益的分布时间序列，支持简约的波动率预测。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: The paper generalizes exponential smoothing to Wasserstein space for distributional time series, applied to high-frequency financial returns, achieving consistent parameter estimation and superior parsimony.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 传统指数平滑只能处理实数时间序列，本文将其推广至分布时间序列，在 Wasserstein 空间中保持经典方法的简约性。通过最小化 Wasserstein 距离一致估计平滑参数，并应用于高频金融收益的分布预测，实证表明该方法比基准模型更精确，为波动率相关的分布预测提供了新工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 传统指数平滑只能处理实数时间序列，本文将其推广至分布时间序列，在 Wasserstein 空间中保持经典方法的简约性。通过最小化 Wasserstein 距离一致估计平滑参数，并应用于高频金融收益的分布预测，实证表明该方法比基准模型更精确，为波动率相关的分布预测提供了新工具。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: financial time series forecasting volatility modeling with machine learning

quick_skim.title: Sparse Tree-Based Aggregation for Time Series Regressions
quick_skim.abstract: High-dimensional time series regressions are often regularized to produce sparse coefficients. We show that temporal aggregation provides a powerful alternative to reduce dimensionality in high-order autoregressions and mixed-frequency regressions. To this end, we propose StarTime (Sparse Tree-based Aggregation for Time Series), a convex penalization method that uses a temporal tree to arrange lags hierarchically from high to low frequency. StarTime then flexibly selects coefficients to be aggregated at possibly varying frequencies, sparse or a combination thereof. We provide new error bounds for StarTime, demonstrate improved estimation accuracy and recovery of aggregation and sparsity in simulations relative to benchmarks, and illustrate StarTime's relevance for financial and macroeconomic applications.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: temporal aggregation for time series regressions, handles high-order and mixed-frequency lags
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 时间序列回归的时间聚合方法，处理高阶和混频滞后
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 时间序列回归的时间聚合方法，处理高阶和混频滞后
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 时间序列回归的时间聚合方法，处理高阶和混频滞后
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes StarTime, a convex penalization method using temporal tree to aggregate lags hierarchically, enabling flexible dimensionality reduction and improved estimation in high-order and mixed-frequency time series regressions.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对高维时间序列回归中滞后项众多导致维数灾难的问题，提出StarTime方法，通过构建时间树对滞后项进行层次化排列，并利用凸惩罚灵活选择聚合频率或稀疏化，实现了维度约简与估计精度提升，模拟和实证表明优于传统基准。

quick_skim.title: Zero-Copy Semantic Contagion: An In-Memory Streaming Architecture for Evolving Attention Graphs
quick_skim.abstract: Per-ticker forecasting models dominate financial time-series work yet remain blind to cross-company propagation: a foundry disruption in Taiwan does not register in a single-asset model until Apple's own price has already moved. To address this limitation, we introduce a heterogeneous Rust-Python streaming architecture that maps cross-company attention as a continuous-time graph driven directly from text. We show that on the ingestion side, a zero-copy Rust edge parses news records in $\sim$100 ns and scans the target equity universe in $\sim$1.2 $μ$s. On the inference end, a multivariate Neural Hawkes Process featuring per-node continuous-time LSTM states and a bilinear latent projection propagates directed excitation, while an adaptive pruning rule bounds the computational cost of dynamic neighborhood updates. Combining these stages, we demonstrate an end-to-end processing latency of $\sim$13 ms per incoming news record on a single commodity CPU. Evaluated on a one-month temporal holdout of the FNSPID corpus (638 articles across 47 tickers), the system delivers a $1.70\times$ precision lift over random at the 90th-percentile next-day return threshold, and $3.36\times$ over a same-sector baseline. Crucially, removing the graph topology collapses precision to zero, confirming that the dynamic attention network is the sole driver of cross-company signal in this architecture.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: financial time-series forecasting using cross-company attention graph and neural Hawkes process
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用跨公司注意力图和神经霍克斯过程的金融时间序列预测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用跨公司注意力图和神经霍克斯过程的金融时间序列预测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用跨公司注意力图和神经霍克斯过程的金融时间序列预测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a streaming architecture that models cross-company attention as a continuous-time graph from text, using a multivariate Neural Hawkes Process to capture propagation beyond per-ticker models, achieving nanosecond parsing and improved forecasting.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对单只股票预测模型忽略跨公司信息传播的局限，提出异质Rust-Python流架构，从文本构建连续时间跨公司注意力图，并采用多变量神经霍克斯过程传播定向激励。零拷贝Rust边在~100ns内解析新闻，推断端利用每节点连续时间LSTM状态和双线性投影。该架构能够捕获财务时间序列中的跨公司传染效应，提升预测能力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对单只股票预测模型忽略跨公司信息传播的局限，提出异质Rust-Python流架构，从文本构建连续时间跨公司注意力图，并采用多变量神经霍克斯过程传播定向激励。零拷贝Rust边在~100ns内解析新闻，推断端利用每节点连续时间LSTM状态和双线性投影。该架构能够捕获财务时间序列中的跨公司传染效应，提升预测能力。

quick_skim.title: TS-ICL: A Flexible Time-Indexed Foundation Model for Time Series via In-Context Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Foundation models mark a profound paradigm shift in time series modeling, with task-specific models being superseded by general-purpose zero-shot models. Yet, current approaches primarily focus on forecasting, while real-world time series are often irregularly and partially observed, requiring models that can jointly forecast, impute missing values, and handle degraded sampling conditions. To address these challenges, we introduce TS-ICL, a novel probabilistic In-Context Learning encoder--regressor Transformer that unifies forecasting and imputation. TS-ICL formulates time series tasks as timestamp-aligned regression and naturally incorporates covariates by training on synthetic dependency structures generated from a novel causal data prior. Empirically, TS-ICL achieves a new state-of-the-art in imputation, while remaining competitive with leading forecasting foundation models across both univariate and covariate-aware benchmarks. It shows particularly strong performance in forecasting with partially observed look-back windows.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: foundation model for time series forecasting and imputation using in-context learning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于上下文学习的时间序列预测与插补基础模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于上下文学习的时间序列预测与插补基础模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于上下文学习的时间序列预测与插补基础模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces TS-ICL, a probabilistic in-context learning Transformer that unifies forecasting and imputation; trained on causal synthetic data, it handles irregularly sampled time series zero-shot, advancing general-purpose time series modeling.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对实际时间序列常有不规则和部分观测的问题，提出TS-ICL，一种概率式上下文学习编码-回归Transformer，统一预测与缺失插补。通过新颖的因果数据生成器训练，模型能在零样本下灵活处理多种时间序列任务，为通用时间序列分析提供新范式。
